description: Reference catalog of non-Wikimedia sources discovered in the user's bookmark dump. Use this to classify any collection-of-beauty files that the Wikimedia Commons API cannot resolve. Each entry describes the source domain, likely copyright status, and how to recognize files that came from it.

sources.subject: NASA imagery (Hubble, JWST, Cassini, astronaut photography, archival)

copyright.license: NASA media usage guidelines (generally public domain, not for endorsement of products)
copyright.notes: Works created by NASA as part of official duties are in the public domain in the US. Non-NASA imagery in NASA's catalog (e.g. ESA/Hubble partnership) may have separate terms.

sources.file_hints: Filenames often contain PIA####, GSFC_########, STScI-, carina_nebula, or 'orig' suffix.

copyright.license: Pexels License (free to use, cannot sell unaltered copies, no identifiable people/logos for commercial without permission)

sources.file_hints: Often large JPEGs with Pexels-style numeric IDs.

copyright.license: Freepik License (free tier requires attribution; Premium tier commercial allowed)
copyright.notes: User-referenced file was a premium-video asset (foam_4087438).
Magnum Photos agency imagery. User bookmarked McCurry's 'Dust Storm, Rajasthan, India, 1983'. Definitely copyrighted; redistribution requires licensing.
Contemporary landscape photographer. Multiple bookmarks reference Kordan's site.

copyright.license: All rights reserved — The M.C. Escher Company B.V.
copyright.notes: Escher works enter public domain in most jurisdictions 2043 (70 years post-mortem from 1972). Museum images are watermarked reproductions; reuse requires permission. User bookmarked many specific Escher plates (Metamorphosis I/II, Belvedere, Relativity, Waterfall, Drawing Hands, Hand with Reflecting Sphere, etc.).

sources.file_hints: Filenames may contain 'escher', 'cat###' (catalog numbers), or work titles like 'metamorphosis', 'bonifacio', 'castrovalva', 'babel-escher'.

copyright.license: All rights reserved — The Ernie Barnes Family Trust
copyright.notes: American painter famous for 'The Sugar Shack'. Definitely copyrighted. User bookmarked: The Sugar Shack, High Aspirations, Springboard, In the Beginning, In the Moment, Anniversary, The Tunesmith, His Effort.

Underlying works may be public domain (old masters) but auction-house photography can carry its own thin copyright. User bookmarks include Chinese modern paintings by Huang Binhong (1865-1955), Ya Ming (1924-2002), Song Wenzhi (1919-1999) — underlying works likely still copyrighted outside China in most cases.

copyright.license: All rights reserved — Fundació Gala-Salvador Dalí / VEGAP
copyright.notes: Dalí works remain under copyright until ~2060 (Spain: 80 years post-mortem for authors who died before 1987). Any Dalí image in this collection should be flagged copyrighted.

copyright.license: Nicholas Rougeux retains rights to his digital remixes; underlying source material (Redouté, Euclid, Werner's nomenclature, Twining's plants, etc.) is public domain.
copyright.notes: High-quality modern reworkings of public-domain natural-history and scientific illustration.

Mirrors both public-domain scans (Redouté 'Les Roses', Gerard's Herball, Sowerby 'British Mineralogy', Twining, Hulme 'Familiar Wild Flowers', etc. — all PD) and some still-copyrighted works. Generally PD for anything pre-1930.

copyright.license: Content highlighted is public domain; the site's editorial is CC-BY-SA.

copyright.notes: Boston Public Library / Digital Commonwealth — mostly public-domain historical holdings. User bookmarked: commonwealth:ww72cb78j, commonwealth:gf06h372n.

copyright.license: Art Institute of Chicago open-access collection — CC0 for out-of-copyright works.

copyright.notes: Treat as needs_review unless specific file can be matched to an underlying work.
Images shared via Facebook CDN. Copyright belongs to the original poster/creator. Cannot be determined from URL alone — URLs also expire. Mark needs_review.

copyright.license: All rights reserved — Rizzoli / Luigi Serafini
copyright.notes: Contemporary surrealist encyclopedia. User bookmarked a PDF on holybooks.com — unofficial scan; copyright still belongs to Serafini.

sources.notes: Bookmarked video URLs — not images. Ignore for image metadata.
Same as facebook above — transient URLs, copyright belongs to poster, mark needs_review.

how_to_use: When scripts/fetch-wikimedia-metadata.mjs flags a collection-of-beauty file with needs_review=true, consult this catalog. If the filename contains clues like 'PIA', 'STScI', 'GSFC' → NASA (PD). 'escher' or Escher work titles → copyrighted. 'kordan' / 'DSC_' generic photo IDs → possibly Daniel Kordan. 'McCurry' or 'INDIA-10841' → Steve McCurry / Magnum (copyrighted). 'Serafini' or Codex → copyrighted. Otherwise mark as unknown and escalate to the user.
